name: JonDash official addons
description: Official add-on modules and helpers for JonDash. In JonDash, add this repo as a module source (Admin -> Modules -> Manage sources) to browse and install. Modules update independently of the JonDash base app. Channels: the main branch = stable; this manifest (beta branch) = beta. See VERSIONING.md.

modules.name: Health monitoring
modules.description: Watches your services with HTTP, TCP, ping, DNS and certificate checks, records uptime and response times, and alerts by email or webhook when something goes down.
modules.notes: Monitoring now starts with the server instead of when someone first opens the dashboard, so a restart overnight no longer leaves your services unwatched until morning. The advanced "how often to look for due checks" setting has been removed - it never controlled how often anything was actually checked.

modules.name: Module template (for developers)
modules.description: FOR DEVELOPERS — a working example to copy when building your own module. Installs to modules/template; open MODULE.md in that folder for a full guide, and AI-PROMPT.md to have an AI build one for you. Safe to install, and safe to uninstall when you're done.
modules.notes: The template depends on nothing again: the scheduler helper and its background job have been removed, so copying it gives you a module that installs on its own. Helpers are still documented as an option. Works on far more versions of JonDash than before.

modules.name: Backup Manager
modules.description: Keeps folders copied to another location — a network share or an external drive — on a schedule, and tells you what it did. Can keep dated copies and tidy old ones away automatically.
modules.notes: A much fuller version. There's now a dashboard tile telling you at a glance whether your backups are healthy, and each backup gets its own page with its history, how long it typically takes, and the dated copies it has kept. A backup that's running now shows what it's copying as it goes, and you can stop it. You can look inside your approved folders to pick a sub-folder rather than typing the path, and ask any backup what it WOULD copy before it copies anything. Scheduling is no longer just 'every so many hours' — you can say 'at 2am on weekdays'. A failed backup can retry on its own, backing off rather than hammering a NAS that's rebooting. You can cap how many run at once so they don't fight over one disk, pause everything at once, and duplicate a backup instead of retyping it. Before starting, it checks the destination is actually there and writable, so an unplugged drive gives you a clear message instead of a copy that dies halfway. Needs JonDash 1.5.2.

helpers.name: Scheduler
helpers.description: Runs modules' declared background work on time, starting when the server starts rather than when someone opens a page.
helpers.notes: Same runtime as 0.0.1-beta.1, with minAppVersion corrected to the 1.5.0 pre-release. Proven end to end: a declared job ran at server start with nobody signed in, and was skipped while its module was disabled.

helpers.name: Files and folders
helpers.description: Lets a module copy and archive folders to another location — a network share, an external drive — within the folders you allow. JonDash's own secrets are never copied.

provides.label: Look at files and folders in the locations you allow
Create and change files in the locations you allow
Delete files in the locations you allow

helpers.notes: Permissions are now enforced, not just described: a module that asks for less than it uses is refused and told what it forgot to declare, instead of quietly getting more than you approved. Adds dated-snapshot backups with grandfather-father-son retention — keep a week of daily copies, a month of weekly ones and a year of monthly ones, and older copies are tidied away automatically. Retention only ever removes folders it created itself, never the newest copy, and never anything of yours that happens to sit alongside them; you can preview exactly what would go before anything does, and every removal is listed in the log and the audit trail. Permission screens now name the actual folders you approved rather than saying 'the folders you allow'. Needs JonDash 1.5.2.
